title: Weekly Intel - 2026-02-08
description: The tech industry's push for sovereignty and control is reshaping both global partnerships and market dynamics this week.
author: Ericbrown

title: How to Evaluate an AI Vendor in 30 Minutes
description: The AI vendor market is noisy and crowded. Here are the questions that separate real solutions from polished demos.

description: The tech industry's old guard is showing signs of strain this week, with notable pullbacks from two of its most prominent players
author: Ericbrown

title: The Expertise Inversion Problem
description: AI tools help junior employees produce senior-looking work. The gap between looking capable and being capable is where organizations get hurt.
author: Ericbrown

title: Weekly Intel - 2026-01-24
description: The tech world continues to grapple with fundamental questions about AI's practical value and economic impact, even as geopolitical tensions reshape the digital landscape.

description: Reality checks for executives navigating AI, tech, and the gap between what's promised and what's actually happening.

title: Competent or Just Confident?
description: A polished sales team doesn't mean a competent delivery team. Here's how to tell the difference before you sign.
author: Ericbrown

title: Weekly Intel: 2025-12-14
description: Consolidation moves in media and enterprise tech signal the established giants are repositioning for 2025. Meanwhile, courts and regulators on two continents are forcing Apple and Google to loosen their grip on app ecosystems.
author: Ericbrown

title: The One Decision That Determines Whether Your AI Project Succeeds
description: Most AI projects fail because teams pick their technology before they understand what they're solving. Here's how to know whether building, buying, or waiting is the right call for your situation.

title: Decision Velocity
description: High-trust organizations decide in three days. Low-trust ones take four weeks; and that gap determines who wins markets.
author: Ericbrown

title: Weekly Intel - Nov 2 2025
description: Tech's power players are making moves this week. OpenAI's circular financing, Nvidia buying into Nokia, and governments pushing back against Big Tech.
author: Ericbrown

title: The AI Confidence Crisis
description: Most organizations have adopted AI, but few have genuine confidence in their approach. The gap between performing certainty and building capability is widening fast.
author: Ericbrown

title: Weekly Intel - Oct 26 2025
description: This week showed how fast the guardrails are falling behind. When AI tools can't accurately summarize news and hackers breach nuclear facilities through SharePoint, the gap between capability and control is getting dangerous.

title: The Agent Hype Will Hit the Same Wall as RPA
description: Jensen Huang and Marc Benioff say AI agents will transform work overnight. The same promises were made about RPA in 2018 and then 50% of implementations failed.

title: The Boring Strategy
description: While your competitors debug their latest tech stack, you're serving customers. Sometimes the most strategic technology decision is choosing the boring technology and strategy.

title: Weekly Intel - Oct 19 2025
description: Technology keeps advancing, but the real story is what happens when it hits governments, unions, and market realities. This week: seizures, protests, collapsing values, and a reality check on AI timelines.

title: The Quiet Work
description: Leaders spend their lives extracting value from everything. But some things aren't meant to be useful; they're meant to be experienced.
author: Ericbrown

title: Decoding AI's $109 Billion Investment Boom
description: Individual workers see 60% productivity gains from AI tools, yet national productivity statistics remain unchanged. The explanation determines whether your next AI investment builds competitive advantage or pays premium prices for concentrated benefits.

title: The AI Productivity Lie
description: New research reveals experienced developers are 19% slower with AI tools, exposing the hidden process problems that actually control development speed.

description: This week's stories show both the promise and the problems as tech giants fight for compute dominance while dealing with privacy questions and regulation.

title: Weekly Intel: 2025-09-28
description: Tech's "move fast and break things" culture hit a wall this week

title: Echo Chambers
description: Social media algorithms create information bubbles that distort the perception of markets and competitors. Here's how to build decision-making frameworks that cut through the noise.
author: Ericbrown

title: When AI Automation Erases Competitive Advantage
description: Companies rush to implement AI without documenting the institutional wisdom that makes them competitive. The result: faster operations but weaker differentiation.

title: The Weekly Intel - Aug 30, 2025
description: Big Tech doubles down on control while AI quietly reshapes the job market for an entire generation.

title: The Three Questions That Separate AI Success from $2.3M Failures
description: Most AI projects fail not because of bad technology, but because leaders skip the strategic questions that separate transformation from expensive experimentation. Three uncomfortable questions can save you millions and actually deliver results.

title: The Boring Work That Matters
description: Why the unglamorous prep work determines whether your technology actually transforms your business or just drains your budget.

title: When the Building Stops
description: The monitors still glow in my home office. They just don't know what to show me anymore.

title: AI Isn't the Devil
description: Stop treating AI like either a threat or a miracle cure. Start treating it like what it is: a powerful tool that requires thoughtful implementation.

title: Don't "AI Wash" Your Strategy
description: Why rebranding existing tools as "AI" creates strategic debt that compounds faster than you think—and what forward-thinking leaders do instead
author: Ericbrown

title: Say no to shiny objects
description: Every vendor claims their tool will transform your business, but most executives drown in solutions to problems they don't actually have. The real skill: saying no to everything that doesn't move your specific needle.

title: The Simplification Problem
description: Why "AI Sucks" (or..."AI is great!") Won't Help You

title: The Hidden Cost of Fuzzy Decisions
description: A single unclear decision in the C-suite creates a cascade of meetings, emails, and wasted effort that costs more than most executives realize

description: Why Your Test Projects Never Scale

title: AI Isn't Revolutionary. That's Why It Works.
description: Forget AI Revolution. Focus on AI Evolution. When practical implementation beats transformational promises, real competitive advantage emerges.

title: AI's (and your) copyright blind spot
description: When AI-Generated Code belongs to someone else
author: Ericbrown

title: What you should be asking about AI
description: AI demos look impressive until customer satisfaction drops 15% three months later. These five questions help you spot the difference between AI that works and AI that just looks good in meetings.
author: Ericbrown

title: AI Strategy or AI Graveyard?
description: Most companies are burning millions on AI projects that solve problems they don't have while ignoring the expensive problems hiding in plain sight. The difference between AI success and failure isn't sophistication...it's simply picking the right problem to solve first.

title: Data First, AI Second
description: Your AI project Is doomed without a solid data foundation
author: Ericbrown

title: The truth about technology strategy
description: While tech headlines chase AI and blockchain, the foundation of successful technology strategies lies in the unglamorous essentials most leaders ignore. The organizations that excel invest in infrastructure, data governance, change management, and technical debt reduction rather than chasing the next shiny trend.
author: Ericbrown

title: Legacy Systems Still Matter
description: Don't discard what's working—even if it's not shiny and new
author: Ericbrown

title: Why Speed Without Direction Is a Strategic Liability
description: As organizations rush to implement AI solutions, they risk sacrificing quality and safety for the sake of speed alone.

title: The AI Speedup
description: Accelerating innovation and disrupting established markets, AI-powered development tools are compressing months of work into hours and forcing business leaders to rethink competitive advantages.

title: The Non-Sexy Side of Tech Strategy
description: While everyone focuses on shiny new technologies, these foundational decisions quietly determine whether your company thrives or struggles.

title: When AI talks to itself
description: Self-Learning AI Systems Create Illusions of Progress While Quietly Undermining Their Own Foundations
author: Ericbrown

title: Creating Real Value with AI
description: Moving Beyond Experimentation to Organizational Transformation. New research reveals how companies are rewiring their structures and processes to capture real value from AI.
author: Ericbrown

title: The Silent Culture Killer
description: Technology decisions made without business input create capabilities nobody asked for, while business decisions made without tech input create impossible expectations. Both scenarios destroying trust and creating lasting organizational damage.
author: Ericbrown

title: Navigating the AI Revolution Strategically
description: While AI adoption accelerates at unprecedented speed, fundamental strategic principles remain constant. This article explores how leaders can apply timeless strategic thinking to navigate the compressed AI hype cycle.
author: Ericbrown

title: The AI Implementation Playbook
description: Bridge the gap between AI ambition and implementation with this pragmatic framework for senior leaders. Discover the five-step process that transforms artificial intelligence from boardroom conversation to measurable business value.
author: Ericbrown

title: Harnessing AI's Potential
description: While AI offers transformative potential, successful implementation requires connecting capabilities directly to business outcomes rather than chasing trends.
author: Ericbrown

title: AI Agents: Revolutionary Technology or Rebranded Hype?
description: AI Agents offer meaningful improvements in enterprise automation, but senior leaders should focus on solving specific business problems rather than chasing terminology. Cut through the marketing noise to determine where these technologies deliver genuine value versus where they're simply repackaged solutions with a fresh coat of AI paint.
author: Ericbrown

title: Leading Through the AI Hype Cycle
description: Every executive feels the pressure to implement AI somewhere in their organization, but rushing into AI projects without proper planning leads to expensive failures and demoralized teams. Here's how to cut through the hype and focus on what matters for your business.
author: Ericbrown

title: The Hidden Cost of AI Experimentation
description: Most organizations underestimate the complexity and cost of AI implementations. Success lies not in choosing the best technology, but in understanding and preparing for the hidden challenges that derail most AI projects. After watching hundreds of AI projects succeed and fail over the past two decades, here's what leaders need to know.
author: Ericbrown

title: Balancing Innovation and Progress
description: In the rush to implement transformative technology, organizations often overlook the power of incremental improvements. Here's how to find the right balance between revolutionary changes and evolutionary progress.
author: Ericbrown

title: AI Agents vs Human Teams: Finding the Right Balance
description: A practical guide to balancing AI implementation with human capabilities in your organization, focusing on where each excels and how to create effective partnerships that drive business value.
author: Ericbrown
